NKJV.1: Dare any of you, having a matter against another, go to law before the unrighteous, and not before the saints?
NKJV.2: Do you not know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world will be judged by you, are you unworthy to judge the smallest matters?
NKJV.3: Do you not know that we shall judge angels? How much more, things that pertain to this life?
NKJV.4: If then you have judgments concerning things pertaining to this life, do you appoint those who are least esteemed by the church to judge?
NKJV.5: I say this to your shame. Is it so, that there is not a wise man among you, not even one, who will be able to judge between his brethren?
NKJV.6: But brother goes to law against brother, and that before unbelievers!
NKJV.7: Now therefore, it is already an utter failure for you that you go to law against one another. Why do you not rather accept wrong? Why do you not rather let yourselves be cheated?
NKJV.8: No, you yourselves do wrong and cheat, and you do these things to your brethren!
NKJV.9: Do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived. Neither fornicators, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor homosexuals, nor sodomites,
NKJV.10: nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor revilers, nor extortioners will inherit the kingdom of God.
NKJV.11: And such were some of you. But you were washed, but you were sanctified, but you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus and by the Spirit of our God.
NKJV.12: All things are lawful for me, but all things are not helpful. All things are lawful for me, but I will not be brought under the power of any.
NKJV.13: Foods for the stomach and the stomach for foods, but God will destroy both it and them. Now the body is not for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.
NKJV.14: And God both raised up the Lord and will also raise us up by His power.
NKJV.15: Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ and make them members of a harlot? Certainly not!
NKJV.16: Or do you not know that he who is joined to a harlot is one body with her? For “the two,” He says, “shall become one flesh.”
NKJV.17: But he who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with Him.
NKJV.18: Flee sexual immorality. Every sin that a man does is outside the body, but he who commits sexual immorality sins against his own body.
NKJV.19: Or do you not know that your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God, and you are not your own?
NKJV.20: For you were bought at a price; therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God’s.

ESV.1: When one of you has a grievance against another, does he dare go to law before the unrighteous instead of the saints?
ESV.2: Or do you not know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to try trivial cases?
ESV.3: Do you not know that we are to judge angels? How much more, then, matters pertaining to this life!
ESV.4: So if you have such cases, why do you lay them before those who have no standing in the church?
ESV.5: I say this to your shame. Can it be that there is no one among you wise enough to settle a dispute between the brothers,
ESV.6: but brother goes to law against brother, and that before unbelievers?
ESV.7: To have lawsuits at all with one another is already a defeat for you. Why not rather suffer wrong? Why not rather be defrauded?
ESV.8: But you yourselves wrong and defraud — even your own brothers!
ESV.9: Or do you not know that the unrighteous  will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived: neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor men who practice homosexuality,
ESV.10: nor thieves, nor the greedy, nor drunkards, nor revilers, nor swindlers will inherit the kingdom of God.
ESV.11: And such were some of you. But you were washed, you were sanctified, you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
ESV.12: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are helpful. “All things are lawful for me,” but I will not be dominated by anything.
ESV.13: “Food is meant for the stomach and the stomach for food” — and God will destroy both one and the other. The body is not meant for sexual immorality, but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.
ESV.14: And God raised the Lord and will also raise us up by his power.
ESV.15: Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? Never!
ESV.16: Or do you not know that he who is joined  to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For, as it is written, “The two will become one flesh.”
ESV.17: But he who is joined to the Lord becomes one spirit with him.
ESV.18: Flee from sexual immorality. Every other sin  a person commits is outside the body, but the sexually immoral person sins against his own body.
ESV.19: Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, whom you have from God? You are not your own,
ESV.20: for you were bought with a price. So glorify God in your body.

NASB.1: Does any one of you, when he has a case against his neighbor, dare to go to law before the unrighteous and not before the saints?
NASB.2: Or do you not know that the saints will judge the world? If the world is judged by you, are you not competent [to] [constitute] the smallest law courts?
NASB.3: Do you not know that we will judge angels? How much more matters of this life?
NASB.4: So if you have law courts dealing with matters of this life, do you appoint them as judges who are of no account in the church?
NASB.5: I say [this] to your shame. [Is it] so, [that] there is not among you one wise man who will be able to decide between his brethren,
NASB.6: but brother goes to law with brother, and that before unbelievers?
NASB.7: Actually, then, it is already a defeat for you, that you have lawsuits with one another. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?
NASB.8: On the contrary, you yourselves wrong and defraud. [You do] this even to [your] brethren.
NASB.9: Or do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived; neither fornicators, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor effeminate, nor homosexuals,
NASB.10: nor thieves, nor [the] covetous, nor drunkards, nor revilers, nor swindlers, will inherit the kingdom of God.
NASB.11: Such were some of you; but you were washed, but you were sanctified, but you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
NASB.12: All things are lawful for me, but not all things are profitable. All things are lawful for me, but I will not be mastered by anything.
NASB.13: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, but God will do away with both of them. Yet the body is not for immorality, but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
NASB.14: Now God has not only raised the Lord, but will also raise us up through His power.
NASB.15: Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take away the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? May it never be!
NASB.16: Or do you not know that the one who joins himself to a prostitute is one body [with her]? For He says, 'THE TWO SHALL BECOME ONE FLESH.'
NASB.17: But the one who joins himself to the Lord is one spirit [with Him].
NASB.18: Flee immorality. Every [other] sin that a man commits is outside the body, but the immoral man sins against his own body.
NASB.19: Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God, and that you are not your own?
NASB.20: For you have been bought with a price: therefore glorify God in your body.

NLT.1: When one of you has a dispute with another believer, how dare you file a lawsuit and ask a secular court to decide the matter instead of taking it to other believers !
NLT.2: Don’t you realize that someday we believers will judge the world? And since you are going to judge the world, can’t you decide even these little things among yourselves?
NLT.3: Don’t you realize that we will judge angels? So you should surely be able to resolve ordinary disputes in this life.
NLT.4: If you have legal disputes about such matters, why go to outside judges who are not respected by the church?
NLT.5: I am saying this to shame you. Isn’t there anyone in all the church who is wise enough to decide these issues?
NLT.6: But instead, one believer sues another — right in front of unbelievers!
NLT.7: Even to have such lawsuits with one another is a defeat for you. Why not just accept the injustice and leave it at that? Why not let yourselves be cheated?
NLT.8: Instead, you yourselves are the ones who do wrong and cheat even your fellow believers.
NLT.9: Don’t you realize that those who do wrong will not inherit the Kingdom of God? Don’t fool yourselves. Those who indulge in sexual sin, or who worship idols, or commit adultery, or are male prostitutes, or practice homosexuality,
NLT.10: or are thieves, or greedy people, or drunkards, or are abusive, or cheat people — none of these will inherit the Kingdom of God.
NLT.11: Some of you were once like that. But you were cleansed; you were made holy; you were made right with God by calling on the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
NLT.12: You say, “I am allowed to do anything” — but not everything is good for you. And even though “I am allowed to do anything,” I must not become a slave to anything.
NLT.13: You say, “Food was made for the stomach, and the stomach for food.” (This is true, though someday God will do away with both of them.) But you can’t say that our bodies were made for sexual immorality. They were made for the Lord, and the Lord cares about our bodies.
NLT.14: And God will raise us from the dead by his power, just as he raised our Lord from the dead.
NLT.15: Don’t you realize that your bodies are actually parts of Christ? Should a man take his body, which is part of Christ, and join it to a prostitute? Never!
NLT.16: And don’t you realize that if a man joins himself to a prostitute, he becomes one body with her? For the Scriptures say, “The two are united into one.”
NLT.17: But the person who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
NLT.18: Run from sexual sin! No other sin so clearly affects the body as this one does. For sexual immorality is a sin against your own body.
NLT.19: Don’t you realize that your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit, who lives in you and was given to you by God? You do not belong to yourself,
NLT.20: for God bought you with a high price. So you must honor God with your body.

WEB.1: Dare any of you, having a matter against his neighbor, go to law before the unrighteous, and not before the saints?
WEB.2: Don’t you know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is judged by you, are you unworthy to judge the smallest matters?
WEB.3: Don’t you know that we will judge angels? How much more, things that pertain to this life?
WEB.4: If then, you have to judge things pertaining to this life, do you set them to judge who are of no account in the assembly?
WEB.5: I say this to move you to shame. Isn’t there even one wise man among you who would be able to decide between his brothers?
WEB.6: But brother goes to law with brother, and that before unbelievers!
WEB.7: Therefore it is already altogether a defect in you, that you have lawsuits one with another. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?
WEB.8: No, but you yourselves do wrong, and defraud, and that against your brothers.
WEB.9: Or don’t you know that the unrighteous will not inherit God’s Kingdom? Don’t be deceived. Neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor male prostitutes, nor homosexuals,
WEB.10: nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor slanderers, nor extortionists, will inherit God’s Kingdom.
WEB.11: Such were some of you, but you were washed. But you were sanctified. But you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus, and in the Spirit of our God.
WEB.12: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are expedient. “All things are lawful for me,” but I will not be brought under the power of anything.
WEB.13: “Foods for the belly, and the belly for foods,” but God will bring to nothing both it and them. But the body is not for sexual immorality, but for the Lord; and the Lord for the body.
WEB.14: Now God raised up the Lord, and will also raise us up by his power.
WEB.15: Don’t you know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ, and make them members of a prostitute? May it never be!
WEB.16: Or don’t you know that he who is joined to a prostitute is one body? For, “The two”, he says, “will become one flesh.”
WEB.17: But he who is joined to the Lord is one spirit.
WEB.18: Flee sexual immorality! “Every sin that a man does is outside the body,” but he who commits sexual immorality sins against his own body.
WEB.19: Or don’t you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God? You are not your own,
WEB.20: for you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God’s.

CSB17.1: If any of you has a dispute against another, how dare you take it to court <sup>ⓜ</sup> before the unrighteous, <sup>[1]</sup> and not before the saints?
CSB17.2: Or don’t you know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is judged by you, are you unworthy to judge the trivial cases?
CSB17.3: Don’t you know that we will judge angels — how much more matters of this life?
CSB17.4: So if you have such matters, do you appoint as your judges those who have no standing <sup>ⓝ</sup> in the church?
CSB17.5: I say this to your shame! <sup>ⓐ</sup> Can it be that there is not one wise person among you who is able to arbitrate between fellow believers? <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.6: Instead, brother goes to court against brother, and that before unbelievers! <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.7: As it is, to have legal disputes against one another is already a defeat for you. <sup>ⓓ</sup> Why not rather be wronged? <sup>ⓔ</sup> Why not rather be cheated? <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.8: Instead, you yourselves do wrong and cheat — and you do this to brothers and sisters!
CSB17.9: Don’t you know that the unrighteous <sup>ⓖ</sup> will not inherit God’s kingdom? <sup>ⓗ</sup> Do not be deceived: No sexually immoral people, idolaters, <sup>ⓘ</sup> adulterers, <sup>ⓙ</sup> or males who have sex with males, <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.10: no thieves, <sup>ⓛ</sup> greedy <sup>ⓜ</sup> people, drunkards, verbally abusive people, <sup>ⓝ</sup> or swindlers <sup>ⓐ</sup> will inherit God’s kingdom.
CSB17.11: And some of you used to be like this. <sup>ⓑ</sup> But you were washed, you were sanctified, <sup>ⓒ</sup> you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
CSB17.12: “Everything is permissible <sup>ⓓ</sup> for me,” but not everything is beneficial. “Everything is permissible for me,” but I will not be mastered by anything.
CSB17.13: “Food <sup>ⓔ</sup> is for the stomach and the stomach for food,” and God will do away with both of them. However, the body is not for sexual immorality <sup>ⓕ</sup> but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.
CSB17.14: God raised up the Lord and will also raise us up by his power. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.15: Don’t you know that your bodies are a part of Christ’s body? <sup>ⓗ</sup> So should I take a part of Christ’s body and make it part of a prostitute? Absolutely not!
CSB17.16: Don’t you know that anyone joined to a prostitute is one body with her? For Scripture says, The two will become one flesh. [3] ⓘ
CSB17.17: But anyone joined <sup>ⓙ</sup> to the Lord is one spirit <sup>ⓚ</sup> with him.
CSB17.18: Flee <sup>ⓛ</sup> sexual immorality! Every other sin <sup>[4]</sup> a person commits is outside the body, but the person who is sexually immoral <sup>ⓜ</sup> sins against his own body.
CSB17.19: Don’t you know that your body is a temple <sup>ⓝ</sup> of the Holy Spirit <sup>ⓐ</sup> who is in you, <sup>ⓑ</sup> whom you have from God? You are not your own,
CSB17.20: for you were bought <sup>ⓒ</sup> at a price. So glorify God with your body. <sup>[5]</sup>

AMP.1: Does any one of you, when he has a complaint (civil dispute) with another [believer], dare to go to law before unrighteous men (non-believers) instead of [placing the issue] before the saints (God's people)?
AMP.2: Do you not know that the saints (God's people) will [one day] judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you not competent to try trivial (insignificant, petty) cases?
AMP.3: Do you not know that we [believers] will judge angels? How much more then [as to] matters of this life?
AMP.4: So if you have lawsuits dealing with matters of this life, are you appointing those as judges [to hear disputes] who are of no account in the church?
AMP.5: I say this to your shame. Can it be that there is not one wise man among you who [is governed by integrity and] will be able and competent to decide [private disputes] between his fellow believers,
AMP.6: but instead, brother goes to law against brother, and that before [judges who are] unbelievers?
AMP.7: Why, the very fact that you have lawsuits with one another is already a defeat. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?
AMP.8: On the contrary, it is you who wrong and defraud, and you do this even to your brothers and sisters.
AMP.9: Do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit or have any share in the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived; neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor effeminate [by perversion], nor those who participate in homosexuality,
AMP.10: nor thieves, nor the greedy, nor drunkards, nor revilers [whose words are used as weapons to abuse, insult, humiliate, intimidate, or slander], nor swindlers will inherit or have any share in the kingdom of God.
AMP.11: And such were some of you [before you believed]. But you were washed [by the atoning sacrifice of Christ], you were sanctified [set apart for God, and made holy], you were justified [declared free of guilt] in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the [Holy] Spirit of our God [the source of the believer's new life and changed behavior].
AMP.12: Everything is permissible for me, but not all things are beneficial. Everything is permissible for me, but I will not be enslaved by anything [and brought under its power, allowing it to control me].
AMP.13: Food is for the stomach and the stomach for food, but God will do away with both of them. The body is not intended for sexual immorality, but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body [to save, sanctify, and raise it again because of the sacrifice of the cross].
AMP.14: And God has not only raised the Lord [to life], but will also raise us up by His power.
AMP.15: Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Am I therefore to take the members of Christ and make them part of a prostitute? Certainly not!
AMP.16: Do you not know that the one who joins himself to a prostitute is one body with her? For He says, "The two shall be one flesh."
AMP.17: But the one who is united and joined to the Lord is one spirit with Him.
AMP.18: Run away from sexual immorality [in any form, whether thought or behavior, whether visual or written]. Every other sin that a man commits is outside the body, but the one who is sexually immoral sins against his own body.
AMP.19: Do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is within you, whom you have [received as a gift] from God, and that you are not your own [property]?
AMP.20: You were bought with a price [you were actually purchased with the precious blood of Jesus and made His own]. So then, honor and glorify God with your body.

MSG.1: And how dare you take each other to court! When you think you have been wronged, does it make any sense to go before a court that knows nothing of God's ways instead of a family of Christians?
MSG.2: The day is coming when the world is going to stand before a jury made up of Christians. If someday you are going to rule on the world's fate, wouldn't it be a good idea to practice on some of these smaller cases?
MSG.3: Why, we're even going to judge angels! So why not these everyday affairs?
MSG.4: As these disagreements and wrongs surface, why would you ever entrust them to the judgment of people you don't trust in any other way?
MSG.5: I say this as bluntly as I can to wake you up to the stupidity of what you're doing. Is it possible that there isn't one levelheaded person among you who can make fair decisions when disagreements and disputes come up? I don't believe it.
MSG.6: And here you are taking each other to court before people who don't even believe in God! How can they render justice if they don't believe in the God of justice?
MSG.7: These court cases are an ugly blot on your community. Wouldn't it be far better to just take it, to let yourselves be wronged and forget it?
MSG.8: All you're doing is providing fuel for more wrong, more injustice, bringing more hurt to the people of your own spiritual family.
MSG.9: Don't you realize that this is not the way to live? Unjust people who don't care about God will not be joining in his kingdom. Those who use and abuse each other, use and abuse sex,
MSG.10: use and abuse the earth and everything in it, don't qualify as citizens in God's kingdom.
MSG.11: A number of you know from experience what I'm talking about, for not so long ago you were on that list. Since then, you've been cleaned up and given a fresh start by Jesus, our Master, our Messiah, and by our God present in us, the Spirit.
MSG.12: Just because something is technically legal doesn't mean that it's spiritually appropriate. If I went around doing whatever I thought I could get by with, I'd be a slave to my whims.
MSG.13: You know the old saying, "First you eat to live, and then you live to eat"? Well, it may be true that the body is only a temporary thing, but that's no excuse for stuffing your body with food, or indulging it with sex. Since the Master honors you with a body, honor him with your body!
MSG.14: God honored the Master's body by raising it from the grave. He'll treat yours with the same resurrection power.
MSG.15: Until that time, remember that your bodies are created with the same dignity as the Master's body. You wouldn't take the Master's body off to a whorehouse, would you? I should hope not.
MSG.16: There's more to sex than mere skin on skin. Sex is as much spiritual mystery as physical fact. As written in Scripture, "The two become one."
MSG.17: Since we want to become spiritually one with the Master, we must not pursue the kind of sex that avoids commitment and intimacy, leaving us more lonely than ever—the kind of sex that can never "become one."
MSG.18: There is a sense in which sexual sins are different from all others. In sexual sin we violate the sacredness of our own bodies, these bodies that were made for God-given and God-modeled love, for "becoming one" with another.
MSG.19: Or didn't you realize that your body is a sacred place, the place of the Holy Spirit? Don't you see that you can't live however you please, squandering what God paid such a high price for? The physical part of you is not some piece of property belonging to the spiritual part of you.
MSG.20: God owns the whole works. So let people see God in and through your body.

NIV.1:
Lawsuits Among Believers
If any of you has a dispute with another, dare he take it before the ungodly for judgment instead of before the saints?
NIV.2: Do you not know that the saints will judge the world? And if you are to judge the world, are you not competent to judge trivial cases?
NIV.3: Do you not know that we will judge angels? How much more the things of this life!
NIV.4: Therefore, if you have disputes about such matters, appoint as judges even men of little account in the church!
NIV.5: I say this to shame you. Is it possible that there is nobody among you wise enough to judge a dispute between believers?
NIV.6: But instead, one brother goes to law against another—and this in front of unbelievers!
NIV.7: The very fact that you have lawsuits among you means you have been completely defeated already. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be cheated?
NIV.8: Instead, you yourselves cheat and do wrong, and you do this to your brothers.
NIV.9: Do you not know that the wicked will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived: Neither the sexually immoral nor idolaters nor adulterers nor male prostitutes nor homosexual offenders
NIV.10: nor thieves nor the greedy nor drunkards nor slanderers nor swindlers will inherit the kingdom of God.
NIV.11: And that is what some of you were. But you were washed, you were sanctified, you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
NIV.12:
Sexual Immorality
“Everything is permissible for me”—but not everything is beneficial. “Everything is permissible for me”—but I will not be mastered by anything.
NIV.13: “Food for the stomach and the stomach for food”—but God will destroy them both. The body is not meant for sexual immorality, but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.
NIV.14: By his power God raised the Lord from the dead, and he will raise us also.
NIV.15: Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ himself? Shall I then take the members of Christ and unite them with a prostitute? Never!
NIV.16: Do you not know that he who unites himself with a prostitute is one with her in body? For it is said, “The two will become one flesh.”
NIV.17: But he who unites himself with the Lord is one with him in spirit.
NIV.18: Flee from sexual immorality. All other sins a man commits are outside his body, but he who sins sexually sins against his own body.
NIV.19: Do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, whom you have received from God? You are not your own;
NIV.20: you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your body.

NRSVCE.1: When any of you has a grievance against another, do you dare to take it to court before the unrighteous, instead of taking it before the saints?
NRSVCE.2: Do you not know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to try trivial cases?
NRSVCE.3: Do you not know that we are to judge angels — to say nothing of ordinary matters?
NRSVCE.4: If you have ordinary cases, then, do you appoint as judges those who have no standing in the church?
NRSVCE.5: I say this to your shame. Can it be that there is no one among you wise enough to decide between one believer and another,
NRSVCE.6: but a believer goes to court against a believer — and before unbelievers at that?
NRSVCE.7: In fact, to have lawsuits at all with one another is already a defeat for you. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?
NRSVCE.8: But you yourselves wrong and defraud — and believers at that.
NRSVCE.9: Do you not know that wrongdoers will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived! Fornicators, idolaters, adulterers, male prostitutes, sodomites,
NRSVCE.10: thieves, the greedy, drunkards, revilers, robbers — none of these will inherit the kingdom of God.
NRSVCE.11: And this is what some of you used to be. But you were washed, you were sanctified, you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
NRSVCE.12: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are beneficial. “All things are lawful for me,” but I will not be dominated by anything.
NRSVCE.13: “Food is meant for the stomach and the stomach for food,” and God will destroy both one and the other. The body is meant not for fornication but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.
NRSVCE.14: And God raised the Lord and will also raise us by his power.
NRSVCE.15: Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Should I therefore take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? Never!
NRSVCE.16: Do you not know that whoever is united to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For it is said, “The two shall be one flesh.”
NRSVCE.17: But anyone united to the Lord becomes one spirit with him.
NRSVCE.18: Shun fornication! Every sin that a person commits is outside the body; but the fornicator sins against the body itself.
NRSVCE.19: Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, which you have from God, and that you are not your own?
NRSVCE.20: For you were bought with a price; therefore glorify God in your body.

NET.1: When any of you has a legal dispute with another, does he dare go to court before the unrighteous rather than before the saints?
NET.2: Or do you not know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is to be judged by you, are you not competent to settle trivial suits?
NET.3: Do you not know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary matters!
NET.4: So if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint as judges those who have no standing in the church?
NET.5: I say this to your shame! Is there no one among you wise enough to settle disputes between fellow Christians?
NET.6: Instead, does a Christian sue a Christian, and do this before unbelievers?
NET.7: The fact that you have lawsuits among yourselves demonstrates that you have already been defeated. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be cheated?
NET.8: But you yourselves wrong and cheat, and you do this to your brothers and sisters!
NET.9: Do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived! The sexually immoral, idolaters, adulterers, passive homosexual partners, practicing homosexuals,
NET.10: thieves, the greedy, drunkards, the verbally abusive, and swindlers will not inherit the kingdom of God.
NET.11: Some of you once lived this way. But you were washed, you were sanctified, you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
NET.12: “All things are lawful for me” — but not everything is beneficial. “All things are lawful for me” — but I will not be controlled by anything.
NET.13: “Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, but God will do away with both.” The body is not for sexual immorality, but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.
NET.14: Now God indeed raised the Lord and he will raise us by his power.
NET.15: Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Should I take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? Never!
NET.16: Or do you not know that anyone who is united with a prostitute is one body with her? For it is said, “The two will become one flesh.”
NET.17: But the one united with the Lord is one spirit with him.
NET.18: Flee sexual immorality! “Every sin a person commits is outside of the body” — but the immoral person sins against his own body.
NET.19: Or do you not know that your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God, and you are not your own?
NET.20: For you were bought at a price. Therefore glorify God with your body.
